signals.reasoning: The geopolitical veto is still active and BTC is already trading in a post-flush state after the weekend selloff, so the better Oracle read is defensive patience rather than forcing a fresh short or calling a bounce long.

ETH remains weak with a fresh daily breakdown, but the move is already extended enough under a live event brake that chasing a new downside entry here is lower quality than waiting for structure to reset.
SOL has not repaired the tape, but the current move still reads as a broad market liquidation flush rather than a clean fresh entry, so the veto pushes this back to watch-only.
TNSR is still trending lower, but the liquidity is too poor and the setup is too headline-sensitive to justify a fresh position under the current macro veto.
FARTCOIN has already given back a large part of the prior squeeze and the price/OI profile now looks more like an ongoing long-liquidation flush than an early new short, so I would not chase it here.
ACE still looks weak on raw mechanics, but the market depth is too thin and the event brake is too strong to trust a fresh short after the drop has already happened.
2Z has some squeeze fuel from negative funding, but the open-interest crowding is high, the notional liquidity is weak, and the veto makes that long setup too fragile to upgrade.

LIT is the cleanest remaining short because it still shows fresh shorts pressing rather than only a completed liquidation flush, and it has enough liquidity for the bearish continuation thesis to still matter even under a reduced-size veto regime.
NOT is already in long-liquidation territory and the notional liquidity is too small to trust a fresh position, so this is better treated as a damaged chart than a new trade.
GOAT has big squeeze fuel from deeply negative funding, but after a 13% down day and very weak liquidity this is not a clean long and it is too damaged to short late, so HOLD is the honest read.

DASH has already flushed hard from a recent spike and now has squeeze fuel rather than clean continuation pressure, so the better move is to wait for either a proper bounce-fail or a true stabilization before acting.
